draft.introduction: 1 Corinthians 12 presents Paul’s theology of spiritual gifts and the body of Christ, arguing that diverse gifts serve one body. It addresses the Corinthians’ tendency to privilege ecstatic gifts like tongues over practical service.

translation.text: Now there are varieties of gifts, but the same Spirit; and there are varieties of service, but the same Lord; and there are varieties of activities, but it is the same God who empowers them all in everyone. To each is given the manifestation of the Spirit for the common good.
translation.original_text: διαιρέσεις δὲ χαρισμάτων εἰσίν τὸ δὲ αὐτὸ πνεῦμα

translator_notes.note: The triadic structure—same Spirit, same Lord, same God—is one of the earliest implicit Trinitarian formulas in the New Testament, though Paul likely did not intend the later Nicene theological precision.
